original_text: Strategic initiatives reveal Apple's expansion beyond core hardware into media and content rights. The company is actively pursuing Formula 1 media rights with a $150 million bid, signaling continued investment in sports content to complement Apple TV+ offerings. R&D intensity stands at 9.43% of revenue in Q3 2025, indicating sustained investment in innovation. However, the company faces scrutiny over its AI strategy, with commentators noting Apple has fallen behind in the AI arms race. Stock performance reflects this concern, down 15% for the year as of July 18, 2025, while the broader market rallied from April and May lows.

changes_summary: Removed K012 from calc_ids as it was cited but not used to support any claim in the paragraph. The stock performance claim (down 15% for the year) is sourced from news chunk C003, not from calculation K012. Only K007 (R&D intensity) is actually utilized for the 9.43% figure. All factual claims remain supported by appropriate chunk references: F1 bid from C005, AI concerns from C003 and C006, stock performance from C003.
